_meta.description: Curated common-name → family/genus mappings for COLLECTIVE vernaculars (one common name → one unambiguous higher-rank taxon). Used by .claude/agents/extractor.md step 4 to resolve common-name-only mentions at the highest unambiguous rank. confidence_score should be set to <=0.6 for entries resolved via this list.
_meta.lookup: lowercase + strip leading/trailing whitespace + match singular OR plural form. AMBIGUOUS entries explicitly signal 'drop this claim' — do not resolve to the genus/family if the name spans multiple unrelated taxa.
_meta.attribution: Hand-vetted seed (2026-05-30) + ESA Common Names of Insects database (Entomological Society of America, list dated 2026-03-04, downloaded via https://www.entsoc.org/common-names). EPPO Global Database (data.eppo.int, https://api.eppo.int/gd/v2/) ingested via authenticated REST API on 2026-06-11; non-Animalia kingdoms only (Plantae, Fungi, Bacteria, Viruses, Chromista). EPPO data is published under the EPPO Open Data Licence (https://data.eppo.int/page/policy).

entries.notes: Green lacewings — Hemerobiidae (brown lacewings) is also vernacular 'lacewing' but Chrysopidae is the default biocontrol context
Closes the Pass-13 Mites→Acari bug class
Singular and plural identical
Mixed clade — use Ichneumonidae / Braconidae / Aphelinidae / etc. when document is specific
Default biocontrol context; other families (Stigmaeidae, Anystidae) named explicitly
Default temperate context — tropical earthworms often Megascolecidae; check document context
Very broad — emit only when document is itself generic; prefer family/genus where possible
Life-stage qualifier; record source_life_stage='larva'
Specific to B. oleracea cultivar group — cabbage, broccoli, kale, etc.
Wheat / barley / rye / oats
Tephritidae OR Drosophilidae — never resolve without explicit context. Drop unless an adjacent binomial is present.
Agromyzidae OR Gracillariidae OR Tischeriidae — drop unless document fixes the family
Brassica nigra / B. juncea / Sinapis alba — different IPM behaviors, drop
Eleusine / Pennisetum / Setaria / Panicum / Echinochloa — drop
Trifolium spp. or Melilotus — drop unless species clear
